meta.title: BMW M550d Engine Replacement Cost UK — Compare Quotes
meta.description: Every BMW M550d engine — N57S triple-turbo and B57 quad-turbo. F10 and G30 generations, real UK costs and reliability. Get free quotes from 20+ vetted BMW specialists today.

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW M550d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M550d engine typically costs £6,000–£10,000 supply only for the F10 N57S, or £8,000–£14,000 for the G30 B57. Fitting adds £1,500–£2,500. Main dealer new engines can exceed £28,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW M550d engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: It depends on the generation. The N57S (F10) is rated ★★★☆☆ — high maintenance, with timing chain, turbo, and oil pump issues well-documented. The B57 (G30) is rated ★★★★☆ — more reliable, with front-mounted chain and improved cooling, but still a complex engine requiring specialist maintenance. These are high-performance diesel engines — expect higher running costs.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW M550d?

acceptedAnswer.text: N57S triple-turbo wear, N57S timing chain and oil pump issues, B57 quad-turbo management issues, and oil dilution from DPF regeneration affecting both engines. The B57 is significantly more reliable than the N57S, but both demand specialist care.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW M550d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept F10 M550d worth £12,000–£18,000, an £8,000 recon is 44–67% of the car's value — assess carefully. For a G30 worth £20,000+, the same recon is 40% — more justifiable. Always assess the rest of the car before committing.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW M550d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: F10 (2012–2017) is N57S triple-turbo, G30 (2017+) is B57 quad-turbo. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N57S timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£6,000–£10,000 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Also check oil pressure — N57 oil pump failure can be equally catastrophic.

eraMap.title: The M550d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original M550d — tri-turbo marvel. Complex, high-maintenance; chain, turbo, and oil pump are watchpoints.
The modern M550d — quad-turbo with 48V mild-hybrid. More reliable but still extremely complex; cooling system and turbo management are key.

rows.bestFor: Insurance jobs, warranty claims; dealer costs can exceed £28,000

replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an M550d engine typically takes 16–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The complex turbo systems require specialist knowledge.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: BMW M550d — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Specialist turbo refurbishment or replacement; don't ignore boost issues
Preventative — catches the fault before it becomes catastrophic
Replace pump and chain together; don't ignore low oil pressure
Sensor, actuator, or cooling system repairs; specialist required
Preventative — replace water pump at 60,000 miles
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair

buyingChecks.title: Buying This M550d? Check These First
buyIf: * G30 B57 (2017+) if you want the more reliable quad-turbo with front-mounted chain
* Documented turbocharger history if buying a F10 N57S triple-turbo
* Evidence of frequent oil changes (every 5,000–7,000 miles) — these engines demand it
* Full BMW service history with specialist M-performance diesel experience
avoidIf: * F10 N57S with cold-start rattle and no chain history
* Any turbo noise or loss of power without diagnosis
* No evidence of DPF maintenance — oil dilution is a real risk
* Poor service history on any generation — the M550d is unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M550d is a remarkable achievement — a triple- or quad-turbo diesel that delivers near-supercar performance. But complexity comes at a cost. The F10 N57S is the more problematic of the two, with well-documented chain, turbo, and oil pump issues. The G30 B57 is more reliable but still demands specialist care. Buy on documented history, and always budget for higher-than-normal maintenance costs.

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW M550d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle on startup (timing chain warning on N57S; check for B57 turbo issues)
* Service History — Including which engine era (N57S for F10 2012–2017, B57 for G30 2017+) — see the Era Map above
* Turbo Health — Test acceleration through the rev range; any lag, whistling, or uneven power delivery?
* Oil Pressure — Any low oil pressure warnings? Oil pump issues are a known risk on N57S
* Oil Level — Is the oil above the max mark? Diesel contamination from DPF regeneration indicator
* DPF Regeneration — Has the vehicle had regular motorway runs? Short journeys accelerate DPF issues
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures; often point to DPF or turbo problems

items.label: * Turbocharger
items.value: Quad-turbo (two low-pressure, two high-pressure in staged arrangement)

items.label: Most Common Failure
items.value: N57S triple-turbo system wear and timing chain — approximately 60% of N57S enquiries [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Average Replacement Cost
items.value: £8,000 reconditioned, supply only [PLACEHOLDER — confirm vs cost sheet]

items.label: Peak Enquiry Season
items.value: Winter — cold starts expose chain and turbo issues [PATTERN DATA]

items.label: Regional Demand
items.value: London (22%), South East (18%), South West (14%) [PATTERN DATA]
